name: Contract End-Date Summary (Next 30 Days)
description: Search Drive for contract docs, extract end dates via Google Docs, filter next 30 days, generate HTML summary table, send email. Tests Drive search + Docs read + AI extraction + date filtering.

phase_a_known_false_positives: Phase A array-index syntax not supported: {{contracts_folder_results.files[0].id}} reports unresolved_ref + cross_step_field_ref errors. Documented 2026-04-10 in WORKPLAN_EXECUTION.md. Runtime resolves correctly.
phase_e_pipeline_validated: step1 google-drive.search_files — folder lookup with computed Drive query (D-B24)
step2 google-drive.list_files — MIME-filter narrows to native Google Docs (D-B26)
step3 ai_processing/generate keyword filter — 3/3 docs kept
step4 scatter_gather over 3 candidate docs (D-B14 unwrap, D-B25 multi-step extract guard)
step5 google-drive.read_file_content — text export from native Google Docs
step6 ai_processing/generate extraction — 3/3 contracts extracted with end_date + counterparty

phase_e_known_open_issues.summary: step7 ai_processing/generate filtered out a contract that was within the 30-day window (LLM date-arithmetic error). Pipeline correct, LLM stochastic.
phase_e_known_open_issues.follow_up: F12 — compile deterministic logic to transform pipeline instead of ai_processing/generate

phase_e_known_open_issues.summary: .docx files in Drive folder are silently excluded by Google-Docs-only MIME filter. Test data was converted to native Google Docs to validate end-to-end. Real users with .docx contracts need .docx text extraction support.

session_bug_fixes: D-B14 regression — unwrapObjectToArray bailout for ai_processing sources removed (compiler)
D-B22 — ExecutionOutputCache stub: clearExecution + getAllOutputs added
D-B23 — CheckpointManager stub: clear + createStepCheckpoint + createBatchCheckpoint added
D-B24 — IntentToIRConverter: computed.op format treated as alias for concat
D-B25 — ParallelExecutor multi-step scatter: isExtractLike applied to last nested step (WP-14 partial fix)
D-B26 — google-drive-plugin-executor: file_types now accepts raw MIME types alongside friendly names
test_data_notes: Drive folder name is 'contracts' (lowercase). IC search query 'Contracts' matches via Drive case-insensitive name= operator.

Folder originally contained .docx Word files. For Phase E content validation, files were opened via 'Open with Google Docs' to create native Google Doc copies (mimeType: application/vnd.google-apps.document).
Test contracts: C1001 (Alpha Tech, 2023-12-15, past), C1002 (Beta Solutions, 2026-06-01), C1004 (Delta Enterprises, 2026-04-25).
